steps.title: Spot the predictable explanation
steps.content: The passage states Rogerson's calculation was more accurate, then explains why—the 1920 method used rudimentary equipment. The clue is that this explanation is expected: naturally, primitive tools would yield less accurate results.

steps.title: Apply the clue to the choices
steps.content:
The logical relationship between the two sentences is concession/acknowledgment. Sentence 1 states Rogerson's calculation was more accurate than the 1920 one; Sentence 2 explains why that difference is unsurprising — the 1920 method used only cardboard and string. The blank needs a transition signaling that the reason is natural and expected.
  • Choice A — "To that end,": signals that Sentence 2 is a purposeful step toward achieving the goal of Sentence 1 — but Sentence 2 explains a past fact, not a purposeful action. ✗
  • Choice B — "Of course,": signals that what follows is unsurprising given what came before — the rudimentary equipment naturally explains lower accuracy. ✓
  • Choice C — "Likewise,": signals similarity — Sentence 2 is an explanation, not a parallel situation. ✗
  • Choice D — "Ultimately,": signals a final conclusion — Sentence 2 is an explanatory aside, not a culminating result. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
